title: Listen and Choose a Response — Academic and Personal

questions.stem: Could you tell me what I got wrong on question three? I thought my answer was correct.

options.A: Let's look at it together. The question tested application, not memorization.
options.B: Question three was the hardest on the exam.
options.C: You should review the textbook chapter.

questions.explanation: The speaker is questioning their grade on a specific item, so offering to review it together and explain the reasoning is constructive.

questions.stem: We're forming a study group for the chemistry final. Are you interested?

options.A: Absolutely! When and where are you meeting?

questions.explanation: The speaker is inviting the listener to join a study group, so an enthusiastic acceptance with logistical follow-up is the natural response.

questions.stem: I finally finished reading that 500-page book for literature class.

options.A: That's impressive! What did you think of the ending?
options.B: 500 pages is quite long for a novel.

questions.explanation: The speaker is sharing an accomplishment, so acknowledging their effort and engaging with the content is encouraging.

questions.stem: My laptop keeps crashing whenever I open the design software. Any ideas?

options.A: Try updating your graphics drivers. That usually fixes rendering issues.
options.B: Design software requires a powerful computer.
options.C: I use a different program for design.

questions.explanation: The speaker is describing a technical problem and asking for help, so offering a specific troubleshooting step is most useful.

questions.stem: You haven't been to class in three days. Is everything okay?

options.A: I've been dealing with a family emergency, but I'm getting back on track.
options.B: Attendance isn't mandatory for this course.
options.C: I've been busy with other classes.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing concern about a friend's absence, so an honest explanation that addresses their concern is the appropriate response.

questions.stem: I dropped the course last week. When will the tuition refund appear on my account?

options.A: Refunds are processed within 5 to 10 business days.
options.B: Tuition is very expensive this semester.
options.C: You should have dropped it earlier.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a specific timeline for a refund, so providing the exact processing time directly answers their question.

questions.stem: The bookstore is sold out of the required textbook. What should I do?

options.A: You can order it online or check the library's course reserves.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for alternatives after encountering an obstacle, so providing practical options directly helps solve their problem.

questions.stem: I heard you won the undergraduate research award. That's fantastic!

options.A: Thank you so much! I couldn't have done it without my advisor's support.
options.B: The research award includes a small stipend.

questions.explanation: The speaker is offering congratulations, so graciously accepting the compliment and acknowledging support is the polite response.

audioSegments.text: Could you tell me what I got wrong on question three? I thought my answer was correct.
We're forming a study group for the chemistry final. Are you interested?
I finally finished reading that 500-page book for literature class.
My laptop keeps crashing whenever I open the design software. Any ideas?
You haven't been to class in three days. Is everything okay?
I dropped the course last week. When will the tuition refund appear on my account?
The bookstore is sold out of the required textbook. What should I do?
I heard you won the undergraduate research award. That's fantastic!
